task_updates.executor_notes: Added `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimeRuler.tsx` as a controlled ruler component. It renders major ticks at `scaleWidth` intervals, minor ticks at `scaleWidth / scaleSplitCount`, translates content by `scrollLeft` instead of owning a scrollbar, and maps pointer positions to time with `(clientX - rect.left + scrollLeft - startLeft) / pixelsPerSecond`, clamped to the ruler extent. Click and scrub are separated by a small pointer-move threshold. Labels use the shared `coordinate-utils.formatTime` helper, matching the existing playback formatter logic. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exits 0.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: The ruler spacing is derived directly from `scaleWidth`, `scale`, and `scaleSplitCount`, while click/drag time conversion is implemented as `(clientX - rect.left + scrollLeft - startLeft) / pixelsPerSecond` with clamping to the available width. Major labels use the shared `coordinate-utils.formatTime` helper, which matches the current playback formatter logic. The component compiles cleanly; visual/manual validation will follow when T3 wires it into the editor.
